metadata.title: Advanced Water and Wastewater Systems Attack Scenario

Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Complex Advanced attackers have been identified targeting water and wastewater systems, utilizing expert-level skills to breach security controls and disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Active Directory OS Process File Network Traffic User Account DNS Record
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method Custom Command and Control Protocol Process Injection Reg.exe Utility User Account Authentication (Mechanism) File Access (Mechanism) Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about water and wastewater systems using artificial intelligence.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker identifies potential entry points and network topology by monitoring network traffic.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attacker creates custom malware to exploit specific vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker exploits a zero-day vulnerability in Ivanti Connect Secure.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker compromises employee devices through drive-by downloads.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.description: The attacker establishes command and control channels for malware communication.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker injects malicious code into system processes.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker modifies system services to maintain persistence.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker elevates privileges to gain access to sensitive areas.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-191: Read Sensitive Constants Within an Executable
stage.description: The attacker obfuscates malware code to evade detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-70: Try Common or Default Usernames and Passwords
stage.description: The attacker steals credentials to move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker maps the network to identify potential targets.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attacker uses stolen credentials to move laterally.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects sensitive data from compromised systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates collected data using alternative protocols.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts critical systems to cause maximum impact.

Scenario.summary: This advanced attack scenario involves expert-level attackers targeting water and wastewater systems, exploiting vulnerabilities, and disrupting operations.
